original_hypothesis.title: Flawed Entropy Padding Destroys Traits for 10% of Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The entropy padding logic appends zeros to the right instead of left-padding. This forces the performance factor to 0 for 10% of all tokens, making them completely worthless for age-based NukeFund claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This statistical flaw predictably destroys the economic value of 10% of the supply. Attackers can use the public entropy view to avoid these 'dead' tokens, leaving them as traps for uninformed users.

proof_of_signal.pattern: paddedEntropy = entropy * (10 ** (6 - numberOfDigits(entropy))) appends zeros to the right

preconditions: Sliced entropy value is less than 100000

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: If sliced value is 12345, padded becomes 123450. Performance factor (last digit) is 0.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker queries getPublicEntropy, skips minting if value < 100000, avoiding tokens with 0 performance factor
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Optimize capital efficiency by only minting tokens that can accrue age-based NukeFund value, leaving dead tokens for others

economic_questions: Exactly how many tokens in the 10,010 sequence are affected?
Does a performance factor of 0 completely negate the age multiplier?
validation_requirements: Write a script to calculate the exact distribution of performance factors across all 770 slots
Verify calculateAge returns 0 when performanceFactor is 0

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Saves 0.005 - 0.25 ETH per avoided dead token

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Creates a class of 'lemon' tokens that uninformed users will waste ETH on

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Total loss of age-based yield for affected tokens
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: 10% of all minting fees spent on dead tokens

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify right-padding effect on modulo operations

validation_result.impact_summary: The entropy padding logic multiplies by powers of 10 (right-padding) instead of preserving the original value, forcing the last digit (performance factor) to 0 for ~10% of tokens. These tokens can never accrue age-based nuke fund value.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The getEntropy function in EntropyGenerator.sol slices a 6-digit window from a slot value. When the resulting integer has fewer than 6 digits (because leading digits were 0), the code right-pads it by multiplying by 10^(6 - numberOfDigits(entropy)). For example, if entropy=12345 (5 digits), paddedEntropy = 12345 * 10 = 123450. The last digit is now 0. In NukeFund.calculateAge, performanceFactor = entropy % 10, which yields 0 for all right-padded values. Since age = daysOld * performanceFactor * MAX_DENOMINATOR * ageMultiplier / 365, a performanceFactor of 0 makes age always 0, regardless of how long the token is held. The age component of the nuke factor is then completely negated. Approximately 10% of entropy values (those in range 0-99999 out of 0-999999) will have fewer than 6 digits and be affected by this right-padding bug.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal at EntropyGenerator.sol line ~134 is accurate. The line `uint256 paddedEntropy = entropy * (10 ** (6 - numberOfDigits(entropy)));` performs right-padding (appending zeros) rather than the intended normalization. The downstream consumer NukeFund.sol calculateAge extracts performanceFactor via `nftContract.getTokenEntropy(tokenId) % 10`, which returns 0 for all right-padded values.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When entropy has fewer than 6 digits (e.g., 12345), it is right-padded: 12345 * 10^1 = 123450. This forces the last digit to 0, which is used as the performance factor. For ~10% of tokens (those where the sliced value is < 100000), the performance factor becomes 0.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
uint256 perfomanceFactor = nftContract.getTokenEntropy(tokenId) % 10;
uint256 age = (daysOld * perfomanceFactor * MAX_DENOMINATOR * ageMultiplier) / 365;
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When performanceFactor is 0 (due to right-padded entropy), age is always 0 regardless of how long the token has been held. This completely negates the age-based nuke factor accrual for affected tokens.

Returns 0 for input 0, resulting in paddedEntropy = 0 * 10^6 = 0. For any n-digit number where n < 6, it correctly counts digits but the subsequent multiplication right-pads, destroying the last digit.

calling_sequence: 10. calculateNukeFactor returns only initialNukeFactor = entropy/40, age component is zero
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path flows from entropy generation through storage to NukeFund claim calculation. In getEntropy (EntropyGenerator.sol ~line 134), the right-padding operation `entropy * (10 ** (6 - numberOfDigits(entropy)))` appends trailing zeros. This is stored as tokenEntropy. When NukeFund.calculateAge (NukeFund.sol ~line 124) extracts performanceFactor via `% 10`, it gets 0 for all right-padded values. The multiplication `daysOld * 0 * ...` always yields 0, making the age component of nuke factor permanently zero for ~10% of tokens.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this issue. The bug is in the core entropy calculation logic.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: An attacker can exploit this by: (1) Reading getPublicEntropy for all upcoming slots to identify which mints will yield right-padded entropy values (performance factor = 0). (2) Avoiding those mints themselves. (3) Only minting tokens with full 6-digit entropy values that have non-zero performance factors. This gives the attacker tokens that properly accrue age-based nuke value, while uninformed users may unknowingly mint tokens that can never accrue age-based value. The ~10% of 'dead' tokens act as economic traps since their nuke factor will only ever be entropy/40, never increasing with age.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Approximately 10% of all minted tokens (those whose sliced entropy falls in the range 0-99999) will have a performance factor of 0 due to right-padding. These tokens cannot accrue age-based nuke fund value, making them significantly less valuable for the hold-to-earn mechanic. The economic impact is that uninformed users waste minting fees on tokens with permanently diminished nuke potential, while informed users can avoid them using getPublicEntropy. This creates an unfair information asymmetry and destroys approximately 10% of the intended token value generation.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the right-padding entirely and use the raw sliced entropy value: return entropy directly instead of paddedEntropy. This preserves all digit information including the performance factor.
Alternatively, if 6-digit normalization is needed, add a constant (e.g., entropy + 100000) to ensure 6 digits without destroying the last digit, while being careful about the 999999 special value.
If right-padding is truly intended for display purposes, extract performance factor from the original entropy before padding, and store both values or compute performance factor differently.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy EntropyGenerator and write entropy batches. 2. Enumerate all 770*13 = 10010 entropy values using getPublicEntropy. 3. Count how many have fewer than 6 digits (value < 100000). 4. For each, verify that paddedEntropy % 10 == 0. 5. Deploy NukeFund with a non-zero ageMultiplier. 6. Mint a token that gets a right-padded entropy. 7. Wait beyond minimumDaysHeld. 8. Call calculateAge and verify it returns 0. 9. Compare with a token that has a 6-digit entropy and verify age > 0.
uncertainties: The exact percentage of affected tokens depends on the distribution of sliced entropy values, which in turn depends on the pseudo-random slot values. The theoretical 10% estimate assumes uniform distribution of the 6-digit window.

If ageMultiplier is never set (remains 0), ALL tokens have zero age regardless, making this bug redundant in that scenario. However, the bug is still a code defect independent of the ageMultiplier issue.
additional_testing_needed: Enumerate actual entropy distribution across all 10010 values to confirm the percentage of affected tokens
Verify that the performance factor derived from padded entropy in deriveTokenParameters matches the behavior in NukeFund.calculateAge
Test edge cases: entropy = 0 (numberOfDigits returns 0, 10^6 multiplication), entropy = 1 (1 digit, 10^5 multiplication → 100000)
